surfaces.removalGate: A replacement operating frame covers setup, validation, workflow, and security boundaries.
surfaces.rationale: The root frame owns repository-wide conventions; Copilot instructions own editor-specific routing.

surfaces.removalGate: A generated scope view reproduces every applyTo binding without becoming a second source.
surfaces.rationale: Instruction files remain authoritative, but their overlapping scopes need one computed audit view.
surfaces.followUp: Generate the instruction scope view during the guidance modernization slice.

surfaces.removalGate: Equivalent versioned user guidance exists and all inbound links are migrated.
surfaces.rationale: User documentation is distinct from repository-authoring and runtime prompt guidance.

surfaces.removalGate: A replacement preserves managed roles, models, interaction types, and invocation edges.
surfaces.rationale: The managed manifest owns roles and invocation edges while agent frontmatter remains executable definition.

surfaces.removalGate: A replacement preserves the model allowlist, assignments, deprecations, and consistency proof.
surfaces.rationale: Definitions are maintained centrally and assignments are generated from executable frontmatter.

surfaces.removalGate: A generated skill view reproduces discovery metadata without hand-maintained duplication.
surfaces.rationale: Skill frontmatter stays canonical; a computed consumer view should replace repeated inventory prose.
surfaces.followUp: Generate the skill consumer view during invocation-graph modernization.

surfaces.removalGate: A replacement preserves all nodes, edges, gates, artifacts, and track routing.
surfaces.rationale: The DAG is the machine-readable owner of workflow order and handoffs.

surfaces.removalGate: Versioned replacements support deterministic migration and package validation.
surfaces.rationale: Versioned manifests separate runtime configuration from repository authoring details.

surfaces.removalGate: A replacement package graph preserves public exports, build order, and clean installation.
surfaces.rationale: Workspace packages are the executable ownership units for contracts, kernel, CLI, and adapters.

surfaces.removalGate: Every persisted schema has one declared source and generated-byte parity before either library changes.
surfaces.rationale: TypeBox contracts and repository JSON schemas overlap in purpose but serve different consumers.
surfaces.followUp: Map generated versus hand-authored schemas before choosing consolidation boundaries.

surfaces.removalGate: Source, bundled asset, and installed consumer boundaries are documented and byte-tested.
surfaces.rationale: The manifest is explicit, but source-versus-shipped ownership is not documented end to end.
surfaces.followUp: Characterize source, bundle, lock, and installed-file lifecycle.

surfaces.removalGate: A replacement maps every validator ID to one executable owner and boundary.
surfaces.rationale: This is the runtime authorization map, not merely a repository lint registry.

surfaces.removalGate: A generated dependency graph preserves command names, prerequisites, parallel safety, and check diagnostics.
surfaces.rationale: The node, node-CI, external, workflow, and hook command lists currently repeat dependency knowledge.
surfaces.followUp: Characterize command inputs and outputs before generating orchestration views.

surfaces.removalGate: Replacement checks retain language-native parsing and equivalent failure diagnostics.
surfaces.rationale: Language tools should not be reimplemented by generic string validators.

surfaces.removalGate: One template-derived heading model replaces hand-synchronized heading lists with parity tests.
surfaces.rationale: Templates are canonical, while validator and summary heading lists are duplicated derivatives.
surfaces.followUp: Generate heading metadata from templates during validator modernization.

surfaces.removalGate: Index-lock coordination permits safe parallel execution with unchanged staged-file semantics.
surfaces.rationale: Serial execution is a documented safety workaround for multiple stage_fixed writers.
surfaces.followUp: Add an explicit index coordination design and representative staged-file benchmark.

surfaces.removalGate: Replacement checks retain stable names, least privilege, exact triggers, and equivalent commands.
surfaces.rationale: GitHub workflows own hosted execution; npm scripts own reusable command implementation.

surfaces.removalGate: Equivalent scheduled ownership exists for every freshness and maintenance task.
surfaces.rationale: Scheduled automation is distinct from required pull-request checks.

surfaces.removalGate: Replacement generation is byte-deterministic and preserves hand-maintained model definitions.
surfaces.rationale: Assignments are derived data and should remain generated.

surfaces.removalGate: A replacement computes every tracked entity count from canonical globs.
surfaces.rationale: Exact entity counts are derived facts and must not be copied into prose.

surfaces.removalGate: Replacement preserves Bicep and Terraform pin freshness with offline validation.
surfaces.rationale: The index is generated cache data; module declarations remain canonical in IaC.

surfaces.removalGate: Replacement tracks source canaries, review dates, and stale references with equivalent diagnostics.
surfaces.rationale: Freshness metadata is a dedicated drift-control surface.

surfaces.removalGate: Issue #13 accepts cutover and every preserved or changed behavior has replacement proof.
surfaces.rationale: The matrix is required through modernization but becomes historical after accepted cutover.
surfaces.followUp: Archive, do not delete, after the final cutover decision.

surfaces.removalGate: Every alias and legacy command has usage evidence, replacement guidance, and a dated removal decision.
surfaces.rationale: Public command compatibility cannot be inferred safely from implementation names alone.
surfaces.followUp: Inventory aliases and command consumers during the compatibility slice.

surfaces.removalGate: All validator families emit stable severity, path, summary, and machine-readable diagnostics.
surfaces.rationale: Shared reporter adoption is partial, producing inconsistent output and remediation context.
surfaces.followUp: Migrate validators by family after snapshotting current output contracts.

surfaces.removalGate: Automatic collection yields representative elapsed, token, retry, subagent, and cache samples.
surfaces.rationale: The aggregator exists, but the current repository contains no telemetry records.
surfaces.followUp: Instrument apex-recall checkpoints before claiming workflow savings.

surfaces.removalGate: A privacy-safe supported source supplies repeatable context measurements or the profiler is explicitly retired.
surfaces.rationale: The profiler depends on local debug logs and is not a portable CI baseline.
surfaces.followUp: Decide whether local profiling remains advisory or becomes supported telemetry.

baselines.evidence: Static frozen-artifact rules pass; the candidate has no workflow telemetry records.
baselines.followUp: Collect representative runtime token and cache samples before claiming context improvement.

baselines.measurement: Recent successful main runs queried with gh run list for ci.yml.
baselines.evidence: Elapsed range 143-209 seconds; median 165 seconds; candidate run 29585231105 completed in 152 seconds.

baselines.measurement: npm run validate:hooks; npm run test:hooks
baselines.evidence: Validation and characterization pass; serial stage_fixed execution has no representative staged-file timing benchmark.
baselines.followUp: Benchmark representative documentation, code, workflow, and artifact commits before hook rewrites.

baselines.evidence: Lock SHA-256 45b3eae971a59165a9374de580140ab92f5e55f1ba6136dce27ebd4c133c597d; workspace package boundaries recorded by package manifests.

baselines.measurement: Reporter import scan; npm run test:lib-json; npm run test:lib-ajv
baselines.evidence: Shared diagnostic library tests pass; reporter adoption is partial across the registered validator command set.
baselines.followUp: Capture output snapshots before migrating remaining validator families.

baselines.measurement: Thirty-day git path concentration plus quarterly drift validators.
baselines.evidence: Recent churn is concentrated in skills, tools, packages, docs, instructions, agents, and workflows; generated freshness, count, and model checks are the existing drift gates.

decisions.decision: Keep skill and instruction frontmatter canonical; generate consumer and scope views.
decisions.rationale: A hand-maintained second registry would create new drift instead of consolidating ownership.
decisions.gate: Generated views match discovery metadata and existing validators remain green.

decisions.decision: Generate validator orchestration views from explicit command dependencies.
decisions.rationale: Package scripts are reusable implementation, but repeated orchestration lists obscure prerequisites and parallel safety.
decisions.gate: Characterization tests preserve commands, outputs, ordering constraints, and required check names.

decisions.decision: Retain serial hooks until an explicit Git index coordination design is proven.
decisions.rationale: Parallel stage_fixed writers race on the Git index; speed cannot override staged-file correctness.
decisions.gate: Representative benchmarks and race tests pass with coordinated parallel execution.

decisions.decision: Derive artifact heading metadata from canonical templates.
decisions.rationale: Hand-synchronized heading arrays duplicate template ownership and create drift.
decisions.gate: Generated metadata is byte-stable and all artifact characterization tests pass.

decisions.decision: Do not merge TypeBox and repository JSON schema libraries until source and consumer provenance is mapped.
decisions.rationale: The libraries overlap conceptually but serve runtime packages and repository artifacts differently.
decisions.gate: Every schema has one source, generation path, consumer set, compatibility rule, and byte-parity test.

decisions.decision: Keep customization source, bundled assets, and installed files unchanged pending lifecycle characterization.
decisions.rationale: Changing an unclear shipping boundary risks consumer drift and managed-file conflicts.
decisions.gate: Source-to-bundle-to-install provenance and rollback tests are complete.

decisions.decision: Retire the v1 compatibility matrix from active gates only after accepted cutover, then archive it.
decisions.rationale: The matrix protects modernization behavior until the maintainer accepts the final candidate.
decisions.gate: Issue #13 closes with replacement evidence for every preserved and changed behavior.

decisions.decision: Consolidate validator diagnostics on the shared reporter by validator family.
decisions.rationale: Stable structured diagnostics improve local repair and CI interpretation without combining validation semantics.
decisions.gate: Output snapshots and machine-readable findings remain compatible for each migrated family.

decisions.decision: Treat workflow telemetry and debug-log profiling as advisory until representative automatic samples exist.
decisions.rationale: No optimization claim is defensible from an empty telemetry set or machine-local debug logs.
decisions.gate: Privacy-safe automated samples cover representative tiers, tracks, retries, cache use, and context.

decisions.decision: Retain public CLI compatibility until aliases and external consumers are enumerated.
decisions.rationale: Removing an apparently unused alias can break consumer automation outside this repository.
decisions.gate: Usage evidence, replacement guidance, and a dated removal decision exist for every alias.
